title: Handshake vs Expandi — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Expandi for LinkedIn automation. See features, pricing, safety, and multi-sender support side-by-side to pick the right tool for your team.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a LinkedIn automation platform built from the ground up for multi-sender rotation. It lets teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard, with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies included in every plan. Handshake's pricing model is based on the number of sender accounts — not per-seat — so your entire team can collaborate without multiplying costs. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale to enterprise volumes.
overview.competitor: Expandi is one of the most established LinkedIn automation tools on the market, launched in 2019. It's a cloud-based platform that offers smart sequences, multichannel outreach (LinkedIn + email), and campaign A/B testing. Expandi charges $99/month per LinkedIn account for its Business plan, with custom pricing for agencies managing 10+ accounts. It includes features like dedicated IP addresses, profile auto-warmup, and GIF/video personalization (as paid add-ons).

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts automatically
featureComparison.competitor: Not available — each account operates independently, no rotation logic

featureComparison.handshake: Automated warmup scheduling with gradual ramp-up for new accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Profile auto-warmup included with smart algorithm limit ranges

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox for all sender accounts — manage every conversation in one place
featureComparison.competitor: Centralized dashboard but no unified inbox across multiple accounts

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan, shared workspaces
featureComparison.competitor: Roles and permissions available, but each seat requires its own $99/mo subscription

featureComparison.handshake: Advanced analytics with campaign performance, acceptance rates, reply tracking
featureComparison.competitor: Detailed step-by-step campaign statistics, SSI tracking, centralized dashboard

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included, human-like sending patterns, smart rate limiting
featureComparison.competitor: Dedicated country-based IP, smart limit algorithms, randomized delays
Built-in email follow-ups as part of multichannel sequences
Available via Hyperise and Sendspark integrations (paid add-ons, $49-$197+/mo extra)

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in A/B testing on all plans

sections.handshakeWins: Handshake's biggest advantage over Expandi is its multi-sender rotation and team-first pricing. If you're running outreach across 5, 10, or 50 LinkedIn accounts, Handshake is dramatically more affordable — $199/mo for 5 senders vs. $495/mo on Expandi. The unified inbox is a game-changer for teams: instead of logging into each LinkedIn account to check replies, every conversation from every sender lands in one place. For agencies managing multiple client campaigns, Handshake's unlimited workspaces and unlimited team seats mean you're not paying per person who needs access. Expandi's per-seat model can get expensive fast when you add account managers, SDRs, and clients. The included premium residential proxies on Handshake also eliminate the need to source your own proxy infrastructure — something Expandi handles with dedicated IPs, but which doesn't offer the same level of fingerprint diversity.
sections.competitorWins: Expandi has been around longer and has a more mature multichannel feature set. If you need LinkedIn + email sequences in a single tool, Expandi handles that natively. The GIF and video personalization integrations (via Hyperise and Sendspark) are genuinely useful for standing out in crowded inboxes — Handshake doesn't offer this yet. Expandi also has a larger ecosystem of tutorials, case studies, and community resources since it's been in the market since 2019. For solo users running one LinkedIn account, Expandi's feature-richness at $99/mo is competitive.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if you're managing multiple LinkedIn sender accounts and want to keep costs under control. It's ideal for sales teams running multi-sender rotation, lead generation agencies with multiple clients, and anyone who needs a unified inbox across accounts. If your primary channel is LinkedIn (not email), Handshake gives you more value per dollar.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Expandi if you need multichannel outreach combining LinkedIn and email in one sequence. It's also a solid choice if you're a solo user with one LinkedIn account who wants image/video personalization features, or if you need the larger community and established integrations ecosystem.
sections.verdict: For teams scaling LinkedIn outreach across multiple accounts, Handshake is the clear winner on pricing and core workflow. At 5 senders, you save nearly $300/month compared to Expandi — and the gap widens as you scale. Expandi holds its own for solo users and those who need email integration or advanced media personalization. But if multi-sender rotation and team collaboration are your priorities, Handshake delivers more for less.

faqs.question: Is Handshake cheaper than Expandi?
faqs.answer: Yes, significantly — especially for teams. Handshake's Growth plan includes 5 senders for $199/mo, while the equivalent on Expandi costs $495/mo (5 × $99). Handshake also includes unlimited team seats at no extra cost.

faqs.question: Does Expandi support multi-sender rotation?
faqs.answer: No. Expandi treats each LinkedIn account as a separate subscription. There's no built-in rotation logic to distribute outreach across multiple senders automatically — each account runs its own independent campaigns.

faqs.question: Can I switch from Expandi to Handshake?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can connect your LinkedIn accounts to Handshake and set up campaigns without any data migration. Most teams get running within a day.

faqs.question: Does Handshake have email outreach like Expandi?
faqs.answer: Handshake is focused on LinkedIn automation. If you need combined LinkedIn + email sequences in one tool, Expandi offers that natively. However, many Handshake users pair it with a dedicated cold email tool for the best results.

faqs.question: Which tool is safer for my LinkedIn account?
faqs.answer: Both tools include safety features. Handshake uses premium residential proxies and human-like sending patterns. Expandi uses dedicated country-based IPs and smart limit algorithms. Both are cloud-based, which is inherently safer than browser extensions.
